summary: ZALA Z-16 drones were used to adjust Russian airstrikes that destroyed a bridge near Pisantsy in Dnipropetrovsk region with FAB-500 bombs, and also to guide Lancet loitering munitions against Ukrainian group targets despite EW interference. In Novorossiysk Bay, a Ukrainian drone attack struck the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen," damaging its superstructure and detonating PK-10 countermeasure launchers, affecting onboard systems including the TK-25 EW complex. Russian forces reported intercepting over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators from the "Rubikon" Center destroying 107 targets on March 6 and 88 on March 7, primarily in Donbas and Krasny Liman directions. Ukrainian developers unveiled new "Lightning" UAV variants with alternative control and retargeting systems, while concerns grew over Russian forces carrying two FPV drones per fixed-wing UAV, prompting calls for enhanced anti-drone defenses.
